thesis: Nucor is the largest U.S. electric-arc-furnace steelmaker, vertically integrated from scrap and DRI through mills and downstream fabrication. **[Fact]** FY2025 was a strong cyclical year: steel-mill pretax **$2.4B**, products **$1.2B**, and **83%** mill utilization. **[Fact]** The company is finishing a heavy capex wave (West Virginia sheet, Kentucky plate, Arizona melt shop) while keeping leverage moderate (**24%** funded debt to capital). **[Inference]** At **~$237**, the market prices mid-cycle quality and project optionality above our through-cycle component base **~$179/sh**.
why_market_wrong: We do not see a clear mispricing edge today. **[Inference]** Nucor's low-cost EAF model, dividend record, and project pipeline may justify a premium to normalized economics, while our base case deliberately avoids capitalizing 2025 peak spreads. **[Inference]** The gap is mostly cycle and growth-capital timing, not an obvious hidden asset.

key_assumptions.source: FY2025 mills pretax $2.383B normalized via midcycle_capacity_value@1.0
[Assumption] explicit downcycle reserve separate from segment multiples

open_questions: Reconcile maintenance capital versus growth capex by segment across a full cycle using post-refresh 10-K extracts.
Update product-level domestic/global capacity map when AISI and competitor project disclosures refresh.
Track West Virginia sheet mill cost, timing, and utilization versus the $3.65B / 3M t disclosure.
Confirm whether 2026 startup-cost run-rate falls materially after Kentucky plate and Arizona projects mature.
